content: Psychosis is a symptom cluster, not a standalone DSM-5 diagnosis. Assessment determines underlying etiology: Schizophrenia (≥6 months with functional decline), Schizophreniform (1-6 months), Brief Psychotic Disorder (<1 month, full return to baseline), Delusional Disorder (non-bizarre delusions ≥1 month, preserved functioning), Schizoaffective (psychosis + mood episode with ≥2 weeks psychosis without mood symptoms), Mood Disorder with Psychotic Features (psychosis only during mood episodes), Substance/Medication-Induced (temporal relationship to substance/medication), Psychosis Due to Medical Condition (evidence from history/exam/labs). Rule out delirium (fluctuating consciousness, inattention). Comprehensive workup includes labs, toxicology, neuroimaging when indicated.
